title: Restart-durability + error-envelope conformance and README
body:
## Goal
Prove the two cross-cutting guarantees the whole API must uphold — restart-durable persistence and one consistent single-field {error} envelope on every non-2xx response with no crash on malformed input — and document the endpoint surface in the README.

## Changes
- {"path":"test/persistence.test.js","assumption":"creates"}
- {"path":"test/error-envelope.test.js","assumption":"creates"}
- {"path":"README.md","assumption":"refactors-existing"}

## Acceptance
- [ ] test/persistence.test.js starts the server with a temp DB_PATH, writes a user + project + task, restarts the process against the same DB_PATH, and reads all three back — proving data survives a restart.
- [ ] test/error-envelope.test.js asserts that a representative non-2xx response from every endpoint group (auth, projects, tasks) is a top-level JSON object whose only field is a string "error".
- [ ] test/error-envelope.test.js sends malformed JSON and unexpected payload types to each write endpoint and asserts the server responds 400 with the {error} envelope and stays up (no crash).
- [ ] README.md documents every endpoint, the bearer-token auth flow, the PORT/AUTH_SECRET/DB_PATH environment variables, and the `npm start` command.

## Verify
- node --test test/persistence.test.js test/error-envelope.test.js (contract)

> 🏷️ Authored with Mandrel v0.0.0 · 2026-07-10

<!-- meta: {"reason_to_exist":"Isolates the QA-owned cross-cutting conformance proof (restart durability + universal error envelope) and the user-facing docs into a reviewable verification unit that runs after the full surface exists (b: risk isolation of the acceptance evidence).","mandrel_version":"0.0.0","authored_at":"2026-07-10"} -->
